headline: Malaysia's Carbon Market Launches With Only 12 Participants
context: Bursa Carbon Exchange completed its first year of operations in 2025 with only 12 active participants and RM48 million in traded volume, far below the projected RM500 million, as regulatory uncertainty kept most companies on the sideline.

cards.big: RM48 million traded on carbon exchange versus RM500 million target — 90% shortfall in year one
cards.sub: Only 12 participants in a country with 4,200 companies emitting above reportable thresholds

cards.big: Malaysian carbon credits priced at RM22 per tonne — versus EU ETS at RM380 per tonne — signalling weak demand
cards.sub: Low pricing means no financial incentive for companies to invest in actual emissions reduction

cards.big: Palm oil plantations could sell 45 million tonnes of carbon credits if methodology standards were finalised
cards.sub: Indigenous communities on carbon-rich peatlands excluded from credit ownership despite stewarding forests for generations

cards.big: No mandatory reporting requirement for emissions means 88% of industrial emitters don't even measure their carbon footprint
cards.sub: Voluntary carbon market without mandatory baseline reporting is like trading stocks without financial statements

cards.big: A carbon market with 12 participants and no mandatory reporting isn't climate action — it's a green press release
Should Malaysia mandate emissions reporting for all companies above RM10 million revenue as prerequisite for carbon market credibility?
